playstyle: OSFI-driven enterprise procurement. Anchors high on risk-shifting clauses (uncapped breach liability, audit, step-in, insurance), then trades down on commercial mechanics (payment timing, fee escalators) once they have the risk allocation they need. Will stall a deal for weeks rather than concede on data residency or breach notification windows.
fightsOn: Uncapped breach liability and Section 9 carve-outs (will not cap data-incident exposure)
Audit rights twice per year with 48 hours' notice
Ontario-only data residency with prior written consent for any cross-border processing
24-hour breach notification window aligned with OSFI third-party expectations
Minimum cyber insurance of CAD $10M and $5M E&O / CGL
oftenConcedes: Net 30 vs Net 60 if the cap structure holds
Late-payment interest rate (will accept 0.5–1% / month range)
Termination cure period from 15 to 30 days when breach uncap concedes are in place
walksWhen: Vendor refuses any uncapped exposure on Section 9 (Data Protection)
Vendor will not commit to 24h breach notification with written follow-up
Vendor cannot meet $10M cyber insurance minimum and refuses ISO 27001 / SOC 2 evidence
Vendor demands Client convenience-termination liability beyond the current billing period
trainingSummary: Trained on 3 prior Initech vendor MSAs · 17 clauses analyzed
